message:
fix: harden lexical cleanup approval + memo GC + readiness serde

Five review findings addressed:

1. lexical_generation: compute_approval_fingerprint is now invariant under
   manifest/shard iteration order.  reclaim_candidates, inspection_items,
   active_generation_ids, and protected_generation_ids are hashed in sorted
   order so filesystem/HashMap-backed callers produce stable fingerprints.
2. lexical_generation: add #[serde(default)] to new LexicalCleanupApplyGate
   fields (approval_fingerprint, provided_approval_fingerprint,
   approval_fingerprint_status, approval_fingerprint_matches, generation_count,
   total_artifact_bytes, total_retained_bytes) so older JSON payloads
   deserialize cleanly.  LexicalCleanupApprovalFingerprintStatus gains a
   Default (NotRequested) to support that.
3. lexical_generation: LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan.generation_count uses
   saturating_add, consistent with sibling counters.
4. memoization: quarantine() no longer double-counts stats.quarantined when
   called a second time on an already-quarantined key.  Guards the increment
   on !contains_key.
5. search::readiness: ReadinessSnapshot.last_search_refinement gets
   #[serde(default)] so older payloads without the field deserialize.

All compile + 62 existing tests (memoization, lexical_generation, readiness)
pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
